title: Loading multiple Images via the Docker compatible REST-API does not work
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**

/kind bug

**Description**

Loading a tar docker-archive with multiple exported images using the docker compatibility REST-API does not work although this is possible using the original Docker REST API and is stated in Podman's API Spec. Additionally, loading the very same archive using Podman's CLI works without any problems.

The problematic lines start with the following line: 

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/b8fde5c5b2a94beac20cd37cab0b59ea0124249c/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images.go#L460

Such an if statement is missing in the CLI command implementation. Having multiple Names is even used for presenting the result:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/17788ed058fdcb15549bdc2b9fa831f9bfa26ca2/cmd/podman/images/load.go#L96

In a local build, I removed the three lines in the API code and then, the import works without a problem. I'd be happy to create a PR for this :) 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest docker.io/library/busybox:latest`

2. `podman save -o test.tar docker.io/library/alpine:latest docker.io/library/busybox:latest`

3. In a second terminal window start: `podman system service -t 0 unix:///tmp/podman-test.sock`

4. `curl -X POST --data-binary @test.tar -H "Content-Type: application/x-tar" --unix-socket /tmp/podman-test.sock "http:/v1.35/images/load"`

**Describe the results you received:**

`{"cause":"2 instead of 1 were loaded","message":"2 instead of 1 were loaded","response":500}`

**Describe the results you expected:**

A successful load of all images contained within the tar archive as specified in the API Spec https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/ImageLoad. Likewise, Docker native API supports this. Since this is the Docker Compatibility API, the behavior is not expected. Additionally, importing using `podman load -i test.tar` works as expected.

**Additional information you deem important:**

In a local build, I removed the three lines in the API code and then, the import works without a problem. I'd be happy to create a PR for this :) 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@flouthoc sorry for being late pushing a PR. Your PR should definitely do it, thanks a lot for taking care! Are there any plans on when this change will be part of a release?
I am not sure if this is gonna be part of `4.0` @mheon can comment on this.
It made it in before the branch so it will be in 4.0
